name: implement-and-review-minimax
description: Implement a task via coder-mid-minimax, then review from three angles (also minimax) and apply fixes with coder-mid-minimax. All stages use the minimax provider only.

criteria: Implementation is complete per the requirements
All gates (cargo test --tests --lib, cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check) pass
Files touched stay under the 500-line cap
No new #[allow(clippy::*)] suppressions — fix the underlying issue
stopRules: Do not commit .pi-subagents/, .opencode/, .pi/, node_modules/, or scratch files
Do not run cargo fmt on the whole repo — surgical edits only

parallel.label: Correctness & regressions
parallel.task: Review the current git diff for correctness, regressions, and bugs. Inspect changed files directly with read. Do not modify project/source files. Use the project-specific rubric from the reviewer-minimax agent (reformat noise, line-limit 500, verify-gate, unrelated-file, untracked artifacts). Report findings by severity (Critical, Warning, Suggestion, Positive). Output a single markdown block with a final verdict: 'lgtm' or 'fix-required: <list>'.

parallel.label: Tests & validation
parallel.task: Review the current git diff for test coverage and validation quality. Are there tests for the new code? Did existing tests still pass (cargo test --tests --lib → 1446 passed)? Are the tests meaningful or do they only test trivial cases? Are golden fixtures (firmware_gen_tests, layer_navigation_tests) still byte-identical if the generator or layer navigation was touched? Inspect changed files directly. Do not modify project/source files. Use the project-specific rubric. Output a single markdown block with a final verdict: 'lgtm' or 'fix-required: <list>'.

parallel.label: Simplicity & maintainability
parallel.task: Review the current git diff for simplicity, maintainability, and code cleanliness. Are there unnecessary indirection, over-abstraction, or dead code? Are public APIs well-designed? Could a future contributor understand this code without context? Is documentation in sync with the implementation? Inspect changed files directly. Do not modify project/source files. Use the project-specific rubric. Output a single markdown block with a final verdict: 'lgtm' or 'fix-required: <list>'.

chain.label: Apply accepted fixes (MiniMax M3)
chain.task:
Synthesize the reviewer findings from the previous phase. Apply only fixes worth doing now (high-value, low-risk). Do not widen scope. Re-run quality gates after fixes:
  cargo test --tests --lib
  cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings
  cargo fmt --check
If a fix would require a separate session (large refactor, new epic, breaking API change), record it as a follow-up in the parent epic instead of attempting it here. Report what was applied vs. deferred.
